oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: SRG-APP-000092-WSR-000055
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: Both the log file and Event Tracing for Windows (ETW) for the IIS 10.0 web server must be enabled.

oval-def:criterion.comment:
Note: If the server is hosting WSUS, this is Not Applicable.
Open the IIS 10.0 Manager.
Click the IIS 10.0 server name.
Click the "Logging" icon.
Under Log Event Destination, verify the "Both log file and ETW event" radio button is selected.
If the "Both log file and ETW event" radio button is not selected, this is a finding.

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: SRG-APP-000099-WSR-000061
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: The IIS 10.0 web server must produce log records that contain sufficient information to establish the outcome (success or failure) of IIS 10.0 web server events.

oval-def:criterion.comment: (Custom Fields - Request Header - Connection) Access the IIS 10.0 web server IIS Manager. Click the IIS 10.0 web server name. Under "IIS", double-click the "Logging" icon. Verify the "Format:" under "Log File" is configured to "W3C". Select the "Fields" button. Under "Custom Fields", verify the following fields have been configured: Request Header >> Connection Request Header >> Warning If any of the above fields are not selected, this is a finding.

(Custom Fields - Request Header - Warning) Access the IIS 10.0 web server IIS Manager. Click the IIS 10.0 web server name. Under "IIS", double-click the "Logging" icon. Verify the "Format:" under "Log File" is configured to "W3C". Select the "Fields" button. Under "Custom Fields", verify the following fields have been configured: Request Header >> Connection Request Header >> Warning If any of the above fields are not selected, this is a finding.

(Default Logfile format: W3C) Access the IIS 10.0 web server IIS Manager. Click the IIS 10.0 web server name. Under "IIS", double-click the "Logging" icon. Verify the "Format:" under "Log File" is configured to "W3C". Select the "Fields" button. Under "Custom Fields", verify the following fields have been configured: Request Header >> Connection Request Header >> Warning If any of the above fields are not selected, this is a finding.

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: SRG-APP-000100-WSR-000064
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: The IIS 10.0 web server must produce log records containing sufficient information to establish the identity of any user/subject or process associated with an event.

oval-def:criterion.comment: (Default Logfile format: W3C) Access the IIS 10.0 web server IIS Manager. Click the IIS 10.0 web server name. Under "IIS", double-click the "Logging" icon. Verify the "Format:" under "Log File" is configured to "W3C". Select the "Fields" button. Under "Standard Fields", verify "User Agent", "User Name", and "Referrer" are selected. Under "Custom Fields", verify the following field has been configured: Request Header >> Authorization Response Header >> Content-Type If any of the above fields are not selected, this is a finding.

(Standard Field - User Agent) Access the IIS 10.0 web server IIS Manager. Click the IIS 10.0 web server name. Under "IIS", double-click the "Logging" icon. Verify the "Format:" under "Log File" is configured to "W3C". Select the "Fields" button. Under "Standard Fields", verify "User Agent", "User Name", and "Referrer" are selected. Under "Custom Fields", verify the following field has been configured: Request Header >> Authorization Response Header >> Content-Type If any of the above fields are not selected, this is a finding.

(Standard Field - User Name) Access the IIS 10.0 web server IIS Manager. Click the IIS 10.0 web server name. Under "IIS", double-click the "Logging" icon. Verify the "Format:" under "Log File" is configured to "W3C". Select the "Fields" button. Under "Standard Fields", verify "User Agent", "User Name", and "Referrer" are selected. Under "Custom Fields", verify the following field has been configured: Request Header >> Authorization Response Header >> Content-Type If any of the above fields are not selected, this is a finding.

(Standard Field - Referrer) Access the IIS 10.0 web server IIS Manager. Click the IIS 10.0 web server name. Under "IIS", double-click the "Logging" icon. Verify the "Format:" under "Log File" is configured to "W3C". Select the "Fields" button. Under "Standard Fields", verify "User Agent", "User Name", and "Referrer" are selected. Under "Custom Fields", verify the following field has been configured: Request Header >> Authorization Response Header >> Content-Type If any of the above fields are not selected, this is a finding.

(Custom Fields - Request Header - Authorization) Access the IIS 10.0 web server IIS Manager. Click the IIS 10.0 web server name. Under "IIS", double-click the "Logging" icon. Verify the "Format:" under "Log File" is configured to "W3C". Select the "Fields" button. Under "Standard Fields", verify "User Agent", "User Name", and "Referrer" are selected. Under "Custom Fields", verify the following field has been configured: Request Header >> Authorization Response Header >> Content-Type If any of the above fields are not selected, this is a finding.

(Custom Fields - Response Header - Content-Type) Access the IIS 10.0 web server IIS Manager. Click the IIS 10.0 web server name. Under "IIS", double-click the "Logging" icon. Verify the "Format:" under "Log File" is configured to "W3C". Select the "Fields" button. Under "Standard Fields", verify "User Agent", "User Name", and "Referrer" are selected. Under "Custom Fields", verify the following field has been configured: Request Header >> Authorization Response Header >> Content-Type If any of the above fields are not selected, this is a finding.

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: SRG-APP-000141-WSR-000076
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: The IIS 10.0 web server must not be both a website server and a proxy server.

oval-def:criterion.comment:
(Application Request Routing Cache - Proxy Enable Setting) Open the IIS 10.0 Manager.
Under the "Connections" pane on the left side of the management console, select the IIS 10.0 web server.
If, under the IIS installed features "Application Request Routing Cache" is not present, this is not a finding.
If, under the IIS installed features "Application Request Routing Cache" is present, double-click the icon to open the feature.
From the right "Actions" pane under "Proxy", select "Server Proxy Settings...".
In the "Application Request Routing" settings window, verify whether "Enable proxy" is selected.
If “Enable proxy" is selected under the "Application Request Routing" settings, this is a finding.
If the server has been approved to be a Proxy server, this requirement is Not Applicable.

(Application Request Routing Cache - Proxy Installation Status) Open the IIS 10.0 Manager.
Under the "Connections" pane on the left side of the management console, select the IIS 10.0 web server.
If, under the IIS installed features "Application Request Routing Cache" is not present, this is not a finding.
If, under the IIS installed features "Application Request Routing Cache" is present, double-click the icon to open the feature.
From the right "Actions" pane under "Proxy", select "Server Proxy Settings...".
In the "Application Request Routing" settings window, verify whether "Enable proxy" is selected.
If “Enable proxy" is selected under the "Application Request Routing" settings, this is a finding.
If the server has been approved to be a Proxy server, this requirement is Not Applicable.

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: SRG-APP-000141-WSR-000077
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: All IIS 10.0 web server sample code, example applications, and tutorials must be removed from a production IIS 10.0 server.

oval-def:criterion.comment:
Navigate to the following folders:
inetpub\ Program Files\Common Files\System\msadc Program Files (x86)\Common Files\System\msadc
If the folder or sub-folders contain any executable sample code, example applications, or tutorials which are not explicitly used by a production website, this is a finding.

Navigate to the following folders:
inetpub\ Program Files\Common Files\System\msadc Program Files (x86)\Common Files\System\msadc
If the folder or sub-folders contain any executable sample code, example applications, or tutorials which are not explicitly used by a production website, this is a finding.

Navigate to the following folders:
inetpub\ Program Files\Common Files\System\msadc Program Files (x86)\Common Files\System\msadc
If the folder or sub-folders contain any executable sample code, example applications, or tutorials which are not explicitly used by a production website, this is a finding.

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: SRG-APP-000141-WSR-000081
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: The IIS 10.0 web server must have Multipurpose Internet Mail Extensions (MIME) that invoke OS shell programs disabled.

oval-def:criterion.comment:
Open the IIS 10.0 Manager.
Click the IIS 10.0 web server name.
Under IIS, double-click the "MIME Types" icon.
From the "Group by:" drop-down list, select "Content Type".
From the list of extensions under "Application", verify MIME types for OS shell program extensions have been removed, to include at a minimum, the following extensions:
.exe .dll .com .bat .csh
If any OS shell MIME types are configured, this is a finding.

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: SRG-APP-000141-WSR-000085
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: The IIS 10.0 web server must have Web Distributed Authoring and Versioning (WebDAV) disabled.

oval-def:criterion.comment:
Open the IIS 10.0 Manager.
Click the IIS 10.0 web server name.
Review the features listed under the “IIS" section.
If the "WebDAV Authoring Rules" icon exists, this is a finding.

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: SRG-APP-000206-WSR-000128
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: Java software installed on a production IIS 10.0 web server must be limited to .class files and the Java Virtual Machine.

oval-def:criterion.comment:
Search the system for files with either .java or .jpp extensions.
If files with .java or .jpp extensions are found, this is a finding.

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: SRG-APP-000223-WSR-000011
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: The IIS 10.0 web server must use cookies to track session state.

oval-def:criterion.comment:
Open the IIS 10.0 Manager. Click the IIS 10.0 web server name. Under "ASP.Net", double-click the "Session State" icon. Under "Cookie Settings", verify the "Mode" has "Use Cookies" selected from the drop-down list. If the "Cookie Settings" "Mode" is not set to "Use Cookies", this is a finding.
Alternative method:
Click the site name. Select "Configuration Editor" under the "Management" section. From the "Section:" drop-down list at the top of the configuration editor, locate "system.web/sessionState". Verify the "cookieless" is set to "UseCookies". If the "cookieless" is not set to "UseCookies", this is a finding.
Note: If IIS 10.0 server/site is used only for system-to-system maintenance, does not allow users to connect to interface, and is restricted to specific system IPs, this is Not Applicable.

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: SRG-APP-000223-WSR-000145
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: The IIS 10.0 web server must accept only system-generated session identifiers.

oval-def:criterion.comment:
(Use Cookies) Open the IIS 10.0 Manager. Click the IIS 10.0 web server name. Under the "ASP.NET" section, select "Session State". Under "Cookie Settings", verify the "Use Cookies" mode is selected from the "Mode:" drop-down list. Under Time-out (in minutes), verify “20 minutes or less” is selected. If the "Use Cookies” mode is selected and Time-out (in minutes) is configured for “20 minutes or less”, this is not a finding.
Alternative method:
Click the site name. Select "Configuration Editor" under the "Management" section. From the "Section:" drop-down list at the top of the configuration editor, locate "system.web/sessionState". Verify the "cookieless" is set to "UseCookies". If the "cookieless" is not set to "UseCookies", this is a finding.
Note: If IIS 10.0 server/site is used only for system-to-system maintenance, does not allow users to connect to interface, and is restricted to specific system IPs, this is Not Applicable.

(Timeout Setting) Open the IIS 10.0 Manager. Click the IIS 10.0 web server name. Under the "ASP.NET" section, select "Session State". Under "Cookie Settings", verify the "Use Cookies" mode is selected from the "Mode:" drop-down list. Under Time-out (in minutes), verify “20 minutes or less” is selected. If the "Use Cookies” mode is selected and Time-out (in minutes) is configured for “20 minutes or less”, this is not a finding.
Alternative method:
Click the site name. Select "Configuration Editor" under the "Management" section. From the "Section:" drop-down list at the top of the configuration editor, locate "system.web/sessionState". Verify the "cookieless" is set to "UseCookies". If the "cookieless" is not set to "UseCookies", this is a finding.
Note: If IIS 10.0 server/site is used only for system-to-system maintenance, does not allow users to connect to interface, and is restricted to specific system IPs, this is Not Applicable.

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: SRG-APP-000231-WSR-000144
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: The production IIS 10.0 web server must utilize SHA2 encryption for the Machine Key.

oval-def:criterion.comment:
(Validation Setting) Open the IIS 10.0 Manager.
Click the IIS 10.0 web server name.
Double-click the "Machine Key" icon in the website Home Pane.
Verify "HMACSHA256" or stronger encryption is selected for the Validation method and "Auto" is selected for the Encryption method.
If "HMACSHA256" or stronger encryption is not selected for the Validation method and/or "Auto" is not selected for the Encryption method, this is a finding.
If .NET is not installed, this is Not Applicable.

(Encryption Method Setting) Open the IIS 10.0 Manager.
Click the IIS 10.0 web server name.
Double-click the "Machine Key" icon in the website Home Pane.
Verify "HMACSHA256" or stronger encryption is selected for the Validation method and "Auto" is selected for the Encryption method.
If "HMACSHA256" or stronger encryption is not selected for the Validation method and/or "Auto" is not selected for the Encryption method, this is a finding.
If .NET is not installed, this is Not Applicable.

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: SRG-APP-000251-WSR-000157
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: Directory Browsing on the IIS 10.0 web server must be disabled.

oval-def:criterion.comment:
Open the IIS 10.0 Manager.
Click the IIS 10.0 web server name.
Double-click the "Directory Browsing" icon.
Under the “Actions” pane verify "Directory Browsing" is disabled.
If “Directory Browsing” is not disabled, this is a finding.

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: SRG-APP-000266-WSR-000159
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: Warning and error messages displayed to clients must be modified to minimize the identity of the IIS 10.0 web server, patches, loaded modules, and directory paths.

oval-def:criterion.comment:
Open the IIS 10.0 Manager.
Click the IIS 10.0 web server name.
Double-click the "Error Pages" icon.
Click any error message, and then click "Edit Feature Setting" from the "Actions" Pane. This will apply to all error messages.
If the feature setting is not set to "Detailed errors for local requests and custom error pages for remote requests", or "Custom error pages" this is a finding.

Open the IIS 10.0 Manager.
Click the IIS 10.0 web server name.
Double-click the "Error Pages" icon.
Click any error message, and then click "Edit Feature Setting" from the "Actions" Pane. This will apply to all error messages.
If the feature setting is not set to "Detailed errors for local requests and custom error pages for remote requests", or "Custom error pages" this is a finding.

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: SRG-APP-000340-WSR-000029
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: IIS 10.0 web server system files must conform to minimum file permission requirements.

oval-def:criterion.comment:
(Verify unspecified objects are not assigned to ACL) Open Explorer and navigate to the inetpub directory.
Right-click "inetpub" and select "Properties".
Click the "Security" tab.
Verify the permissions for the following users; if the permissions are less restrictive, this is a finding.
System: Full control Administrators: Full control TrustedInstaller: Full control ALL APPLICATION PACKAGES (built-in security group): Read and execute ALL RESTRICTED APPLICATION PACKAGES (built-in security group): Read and execute Users: Read and execute, list folder contents CREATOR OWNER: Full Control, Subfolders and files only

(File Perm assigned to Users group for inetpub directory) Open Explorer and navigate to the inetpub directory.
Right-click "inetpub" and select "Properties".
Click the "Security" tab.
Verify the permissions for the following users; if the permissions are less restrictive, this is a finding.
System: Full control Administrators: Full control TrustedInstaller: Full control ALL APPLICATION PACKAGES (built-in security group): Read and execute ALL RESTRICTED APPLICATION PACKAGES (built-in security group): Read and execute Users: Read and execute, list folder contents CREATOR OWNER: Full Control, Subfolders and files only

(File Perm assigned to ALL APPLICATION PACKAGES for inetpub directory) Open Explorer and navigate to the inetpub directory.
Right-click "inetpub" and select "Properties".
Click the "Security" tab.
Verify the permissions for the following users; if the permissions are less restrictive, this is a finding.
System: Full control Administrators: Full control TrustedInstaller: Full control ALL APPLICATION PACKAGES (built-in security group): Read and execute ALL RESTRICTED APPLICATION PACKAGES (built-in security group): Read and execute Users: Read and execute, list folder contents CREATOR OWNER: Full Control, Subfolders and files only

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: SRG-APP-000357-WSR-000150
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: The IIS 10.0 web server must use a logging mechanism configured to allocate log record storage capacity large enough to accommodate the logging requirements of the IIS 10.0 web server.

oval-def:criterion.comment:
Open the IIS 10.0 Manager.
Click the IIS 10.0 web server name.
Under "IIS" double-click the "Logging" icon.
In the "Logging" configuration box, determine the "Directory:" to which the "W3C" logging is being written.
Confirm with the System Administrator that the designated log path is of sufficient size to maintain the logging.
Under "Log File Rollover", verify "Do not create new log files" is not selected.
Verify a schedule is configured to rollover log files on a regular basis.
Consult with the System Administrator to determine if there is a documented process for moving the log files off of the IIS 10.0 web server to another logging device.
If the designated logging path device is not of sufficient space to maintain all log files, and there is not a schedule to rollover files on a regular basis, this is a finding.

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: SRG-APP-000383-WSR-000175
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: The Internet Printing Protocol (IPP) must be disabled on the IIS 10.0 web server.

oval-def:criterion.comment:
If the Print Services role and the Internet Printing role are not installed, this check is Not Applicable.
Navigate to the following directory:
%windir%\web\printers
If this folder exists, this is a finding.
Determine whether Internet Printing is enabled:
Click “Start”, click “Administrative Tools”, and then click “Server Manager”.
Expand the roles node, right-click “Print Services”, and then select “Remove Roles Services”.
If the Internet Printing option is enabled, this is a finding.

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: SRG-APP-000435-WSR-000148
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: The IIS 10.0 web server must be tuned to handle the operational requirements of the hosted application.

oval-def:criterion.comment:
If the IIS 10.0 web server is not hosting any applications, this is Not Applicable.
If the IIS 10.0 web server is hosting applications, consult with the System Administrator to determine risk analysis performed when the application was written and deployed to the IIS 10.0 web server.
Obtain documentation on the configuration.
Verify, at a minimum, the following tuning settings in the registry.
Access the IIS 10.0 web server registry.
Verify the following keys are present and configured. The required setting depends upon the requirements of the application.
Recommended settings are not provided as these settings must be explicitly configured to show a conscientious tuning has been made.
Navigate to HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\HTTP\Parameters\ "URIEnableCache" "UriMaxUriBytes" "UriScavengerPeriod"
If explicit settings are not configured for "URIEnableCache", "UriMaxUriBytes" and "UriScavengerPeriod", this is a finding.

If the IIS 10.0 web server is not hosting any applications, this is Not Applicable.
If the IIS 10.0 web server is hosting applications, consult with the System Administrator to determine risk analysis performed when the application was written and deployed to the IIS 10.0 web server.
Obtain documentation on the configuration.
Verify, at a minimum, the following tuning settings in the registry.
Access the IIS 10.0 web server registry.
Verify the following keys are present and configured. The required setting depends upon the requirements of the application.
Recommended settings are not provided as these settings must be explicitly configured to show a conscientious tuning has been made.
Navigate to HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\HTTP\Parameters\ "URIEnableCache" "UriMaxUriBytes" "UriScavengerPeriod"
If explicit settings are not configured for "URIEnableCache", "UriMaxUriBytes" and "UriScavengerPeriod", this is a finding.

If the IIS 10.0 web server is not hosting any applications, this is Not Applicable.
If the IIS 10.0 web server is hosting applications, consult with the System Administrator to determine risk analysis performed when the application was written and deployed to the IIS 10.0 web server.
Obtain documentation on the configuration.
Verify, at a minimum, the following tuning settings in the registry.
Access the IIS 10.0 web server registry.
Verify the following keys are present and configured. The required setting depends upon the requirements of the application.
Recommended settings are not provided as these settings must be explicitly configured to show a conscientious tuning has been made.
Navigate to HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\HTTP\Parameters\ "URIEnableCache" "UriMaxUriBytes" "UriScavengerPeriod"
If explicit settings are not configured for "URIEnableCache", "UriMaxUriBytes" and "UriScavengerPeriod", this is a finding.

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: SRG-APP-000439-WSR-000152
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: IIS 10.0 web server session IDs must be sent to the client using TLS.

oval-def:criterion.comment:
Open the IIS 10.0 Manager.
Click the IIS 10.0 web server name.
Under the "Management" section, double-click the "Configuration Editor" icon.
From the "Section:" drop-down list, select "system.webServer/asp".
Expand the "session" section.
Verify the "keepSessionIdSecure" is set to "True".
If the "keepSessionIdSecure" is not set to "True", this is a finding.

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: SRG-APP-000439-WSR-000156
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: An IIS 10.0 web server must maintain the confidentiality of controlled information during transmission through the use of an approved Transport Layer Security (TLS) version.

oval-def:criterion.comment:
(TLS 1.2 DisabledByDefault set to 0) Access the IIS 10.0 Web Server.
Access an administrator command prompt and type "regedit <enter>" to access the server's registry.
Navigate to: HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\SecurityProviders\SCHANNEL\Protocols\TLS 1.2\Server
Verify a REG_DWORD value of "0" for "DisabledByDefault" Verify a REG_DWORD value of "0" for "Enabled"
Navigate to: HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\SecurityProviders\SCHANNEL\Protocols\TLS 1.0\Server
HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\SecurityProviders\SCHANNEL\Protocols\TLS 1.1\Server
HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\SecurityProviders\SCHANNEL\Protocols\SSL 2.0\Server
HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\SecurityProviders\SCHANNEL\Protocols\SSL 3.0\Server
Verify a REG_DWORD value of "1" for "DisabledByDefault" Verify a REG_DWORD value of "0" for "Enabled"
If any of the respective registry paths do not exist or are configured with the wrong value, this is a finding.

(TLS 1.2 Enabled set to 1) Access the IIS 10.0 Web Server.
Access an administrator command prompt and type "regedit <enter>" to access the server's registry.
Navigate to: HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\SecurityProviders\SCHANNEL\Protocols\TLS 1.2\Server
Verify a REG_DWORD value of "0" for "DisabledByDefault" Verify a REG_DWORD value of "0" for "Enabled"
Navigate to: HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\SecurityProviders\SCHANNEL\Protocols\TLS 1.0\Server
HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\SecurityProviders\SCHANNEL\Protocols\TLS 1.1\Server
HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\SecurityProviders\SCHANNEL\Protocols\SSL 2.0\Server
HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\SecurityProviders\SCHANNEL\Protocols\SSL 3.0\Server
Verify a REG_DWORD value of "1" for "DisabledByDefault" Verify a REG_DWORD value of "0" for "Enabled"
If any of the respective registry paths do not exist or are configured with the wrong value, this is a finding.

(TLS 1.1 Disabled) Access the IIS 10.0 Web Server.
Access an administrator command prompt and type "regedit <enter>" to access the server's registry.
Navigate to: HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\SecurityProviders\SCHANNEL\Protocols\TLS 1.2\Server
Verify a REG_DWORD value of "0" for "DisabledByDefault" Verify a REG_DWORD value of "0" for "Enabled"
Navigate to: HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\SecurityProviders\SCHANNEL\Protocols\TLS 1.0\Server
HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\SecurityProviders\SCHANNEL\Protocols\TLS 1.1\Server
HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\SecurityProviders\SCHANNEL\Protocols\SSL 2.0\Server
HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\SecurityProviders\SCHANNEL\Protocols\SSL 3.0\Server
Verify a REG_DWORD value of "1" for "DisabledByDefault" Verify a REG_DWORD value of "0" for "Enabled"
If any of the respective registry paths do not exist or are configured with the wrong value, this is a finding.

HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\SecurityProviders\SCHANNEL\Protocols\TLS 1.1\Server
HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\SecurityProviders\SCHANNEL\Protocols\SSL 2.0\Server

Verify a REG_DWORD value of "0" for "DisabledByDefault" Verify a REG_DWORD value of "0" for "Enabled"

HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\SecurityProviders\SCHANNEL\Protocols\TLS 1.1\Server
HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\SecurityProviders\SCHANNEL\Protocols\SSL 2.0\Server

Access an administrator command prompt and type "regedit <enter>" to access the server's registry.
Navigate to: HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\SecurityProviders\SCHANNEL\Protocols\TLS 1.2\Server
Verify a REG_DWORD value of "0" for "DisabledByDefault" Verify a REG_DWORD value of "0" for "Enabled"
Navigate to: HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\SecurityProviders\SCHANNEL\Protocols\TLS 1.0\Server
HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\SecurityProviders\SCHANNEL\Protocols\TLS 1.1\Server
HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\SecurityProviders\SCHANNEL\Protocols\SSL 2.0\Server
HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\SecurityProviders\SCHANNEL\Protocols\SSL 3.0\Server
Verify a REG_DWORD value of "1" for "DisabledByDefault" Verify a REG_DWORD value of "0" for "Enabled"
If any of the respective registry paths do not exist or are configured with the wrong value, this is a finding.

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: SRG-APP-000516-WSR-000174
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: Unspecified file extensions on a production IIS 10.0 web server must be removed.

oval-def:criterion.comment:
Open the IIS 10.0 Manager.
Click the IIS 10.0 web server name.
Double-click the "ISAPI and CGI restrictions" icon.
Click “Edit Feature Settings".
Verify the "Allow unspecified CGI modules" and the "Allow unspecified ISAPI modules" check boxes are NOT checked.
If either or both of the "Allow unspecified CGI modules" and the "Allow unspecified ISAPI modules" check boxes are checked, this is a finding.

Open the IIS 10.0 Manager.
Click the IIS 10.0 web server name.
Double-click the "ISAPI and CGI restrictions" icon.
Click “Edit Feature Settings".
Verify the "Allow unspecified CGI modules" and the "Allow unspecified ISAPI modules" check boxes are NOT checked.
If either or both of the "Allow unspecified CGI modules" and the "Allow unspecified ISAPI modules" check boxes are checked, this is a finding.

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: SRG-APP-000516-WSR-000174
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: The IIS 10.0 web server must have a global authorization rule configured to restrict access.

oval-def:criterion.comment:
Note: If ASP.NET is not installed, this is Not Applicable. Note: If the Server is hosting Microsoft SharePoint, this is Not Applicable. Note: If the server is hosting WSUS, this is Not Applicable. Note: If the server is hosting Exchange, this is Not Applicable
Note: If the server is public facing, this is Not Applicable
Open the IIS 10.0 Manager.
Click the IIS 10.0 web server name.
Double-click the ".NET Authorization Rules" icon.
Ensure "All Users" is set to "Allow", and "Anonymous Users" is set to "Deny" otherwise, this is a finding. If any other rules are present, this is a finding.

Note: If ASP.NET is not installed, this is Not Applicable. Note: If the Server is hosting Microsoft SharePoint, this is Not Applicable. Note: If the server is hosting WSUS, this is Not Applicable. Note: If the server is hosting Exchange, this is Not Applicable
Note: If the server is public facing, this is Not Applicable If the website is not behind a load balancer or proxy server, this is Not Applicable.
Open the IIS 10.0 Manager.
Click the IIS 10.0 web server name.
Double-click the ".NET Authorization Rules" icon.
Ensure "All Users" is set to "Allow", and "Anonymous Users" is set to "Deny" otherwise, this is a finding. If any other rules are present, this is a finding.

Note: If ASP.NET is not installed, this is Not Applicable. Note: If the Server is hosting Microsoft SharePoint, this is Not Applicable. Note: If the server is hosting WSUS, this is Not Applicable. Note: If the server is hosting Exchange, this is Not Applicable
Note: If the server is public facing, this is Not Applicable If the website is not behind a load balancer or proxy server, this is Not Applicable.

Click the IIS 10.0 web server name.
Double-click the ".NET Authorization Rules" icon.
Ensure "All Users" is set to "Allow", and "Anonymous Users" is set to "Deny" otherwise, this is a finding. If any other rules are present, this is a finding.

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: SRG-APP-000001-WSR-000001
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: The IIS 10.0 websites MaxConnections setting must be configured to limit the number of allowed simultaneous session requests.

oval-def:criterion.comment:
Access the IIS 10.0 IIS Manager.
Click the IIS 10.0 server.
Select "Configuration Editor" under the "Management" section.
From the "Section:" drop-down list at the top of the configuration editor, locate "system.applicationHost/sites".
Expand "siteDefaults". Expand "limits".
Review the results and verify the value is greater than zero for the "maxconnections" parameter.
If the maxconnections parameter is set to zero, this is a finding.

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: SRG-APP-000516-WSR-000174
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: The IIS 10.0 web server must enable HTTP Strict Transport Security (HSTS).

oval-def:criterion.comment:
Access the IIS 10.0 Web Server. Open IIS Manager. Click the IIS 10.0 web server name. Open on Configuration Editor under Management. For the Section, navigate to system.applicationHost/sites. Expand siteDefaults and HSTS. If enabled is not set to True, this is a finding. If includeSubDomains is not set to True, this is a finding. If max-age is not set to a value greater than 0, this is a finding. If redirectHttpToHttps is not True, this is a finding.
If the website is behind a load balancer or proxy server, and HSTS enablement is handled there, this is Not Applicable.
If the version of Windows Server does not natively support HSTS, this is not a finding.

Access the IIS 10.0 Web Server. Open IIS Manager. Click the IIS 10.0 web server name. Open on Configuration Editor under Management. For the Section, navigate to system.applicationHost/sites. Expand siteDefaults and HSTS. If enabled is not set to True, this is a finding. If includeSubDomains is not set to True, this is a finding. If max-age is not set to a value greater than 0, this is a finding. If redirectHttpToHttps is not True, this is a finding.
If the website is behind a load balancer or proxy server, and HSTS enablement is handled there, this is Not Applicable.
If the version of Windows Server does not natively support HSTS, this is not a finding.

Access the IIS 10.0 Web Server. Open IIS Manager. Click the IIS 10.0 web server name. Open on Configuration Editor under Management. For the Section, navigate to system.applicationHost/sites. Expand siteDefaults and HSTS. If enabled is not set to True, this is a finding. If includeSubDomains is not set to True, this is a finding. If max-age is not set to a value greater than 0, this is a finding. If redirectHttpToHttps is not True, this is a finding.
If the website is behind a load balancer or proxy server, and HSTS enablement is handled there, this is Not Applicable.
If the version of Windows Server does not natively support HSTS, this is not a finding.

Access the IIS 10.0 Web Server. Open IIS Manager. Click the IIS 10.0 web server name. Open on Configuration Editor under Management. For the Section, navigate to system.applicationHost/sites. Expand siteDefaults and HSTS. If enabled is not set to True, this is a finding. If includeSubDomains is not set to True, this is a finding. If max-age is not set to a value greater than 0, this is a finding. If redirectHttpToHttps is not True, this is a finding.
If the website is behind a load balancer or proxy server, and HSTS enablement is handled there, this is Not Applicable.
If the version of Windows Server does not natively support HSTS, this is not a finding.

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: SRG-APP-000266-WSR-000159
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: HTTPAPI Server version must be removed from the HTTP Response Header information.

oval-def:criterion.comment:
Open Registry Editor.
Navigate to “HKLM\System\CurrentControlSet\Services\HTTP\Parameters”
Verify “DisableServerHeader” is set to “2”.
If REG_DWORD DisableServerHeader is not set to 2, this is a finding.
If the System Administrator can show that Server Version information has been removed via other means, such as using a rewrite outbound rule, this is not a finding.

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: SRG-APP-000266-WSR-000159
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: ASP.NET version must be removed from the HTTP Response Header information.

oval-def:criterion.comment:
Note: If ASP.NET is not installed, this is Not Applicable.
Open the IIS 10.0 Manager.
Under the "Connections" pane on the left side of the management console, select the IIS 10.0 web server.
Click the HTTP Response Headers button.
Click to select the “X-Powered-By” HTTP Header.
If “X-Powered-By” has not been removed, this is a finding.

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: SRG-APP-000141-WSR-000015
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: The Request Smuggling filter must be enabled.

oval-def:criterion.comment:
Open Registry Editor. Navigate to 'HKLM\System\CurrentControlSet\Services\HTTP\Parameters' Verify 'DisableRequestSmuggling' is set to '1'.
If REG_DWORD DisableRequestSmuggling is not set to 1, this is a finding.

Is WSUS Server Installed?
Is Exchange Server Installed?

Management Service - Enable remote connections

win-def:appcmdlistconfig_test.comment: (Date Field) Open the IIS 10.0 Manager. Click the IIS 10.0 web server name. Click the Logging icon. Under Format select W3C. Click Select Fields, verify at a minimum the following fields are checked: Date, Time, Client IP Address, User Name, Method, URI Query, Protocol Status, and Referrer. If not, this is a finding.

(Time Field) Open the IIS 10.0 Manager. Click the IIS 10.0 web server name. Click the Logging icon. Under Format select W3C. Click Select Fields, verify at a minimum the following fields are checked: Date, Time, Client IP Address, User Name, Method, URI Query, Protocol Status, and Referrer. If not, this is a finding.

(Client IP Address Field) Open the IIS 10.0 Manager. Click the IIS 10.0 web server name. Click the Logging icon. Under Format select W3C. Click Select Fields, verify at a minimum the following fields are checked: Date, Time, Client IP Address, User Name, Method, URI Query, Protocol Status, and Referrer. If not, this is a finding.

(User Name Field) Open the IIS 10.0 Manager. Click the IIS 10.0 web server name. Click the Logging icon. Under Format select W3C. Click Select Fields, verify at a minimum the following fields are checked: Date, Time, Client IP Address, User Name, Method, URI Query, Protocol Status, and Referrer. If not, this is a finding.

(Method Field) Open the IIS 10.0 Manager. Click the IIS 10.0 web server name. Click the Logging icon. Under Format select W3C. Click Select Fields, verify at a minimum the following fields are checked: Date, Time, Client IP Address, User Name, Method, URI Query, Protocol Status, and Referrer. If not, this is a finding.

(URI Query Field) Open the IIS 10.0 Manager. Click the IIS 10.0 web server name. Click the Logging icon. Under Format select W3C. Click Select Fields, verify at a minimum the following fields are checked: Date, Time, Client IP Address, User Name, Method, URI Query, Protocol Status, and Referrer. If not, this is a finding.

(Protocol Status Field) Open the IIS 10.0 Manager. Click the IIS 10.0 web server name. Click the Logging icon. Under Format select W3C. Click Select Fields, verify at a minimum the following fields are checked: Date, Time, Client IP Address, User Name, Method, URI Query, Protocol Status, and Referrer. If not, this is a finding.

(Referrer Field) Open the IIS 10.0 Manager. Click the IIS 10.0 web server name. Click the Logging icon. Under Format select W3C. Click Select Fields, verify at a minimum the following fields are checked: Date, Time, Client IP Address, User Name, Method, URI Query, Protocol Status, and Referrer. If not, this is a finding.
Both the log file and Event Tracing for Windows (ETW) for the IIS 10.0 web server must be enabled.

win-def:cmdlet_test.comment: (Custom Fields - Request Header - Connection) Access the IIS 10.0 web server IIS Manager. Click the IIS 10.0 web server name. Under IIS, double-click the Logging icon. Verify the Format: under Log File is configured to W3C. Select the Fields button. Under Custom Fields, verify the following fields have been configured: Request Header - Connection Request Header - Warning Server Variable - HTTP_CONNECTION If any of the above fields are not selected, this is a finding.

(Custom Fields - Request Header - Warning) Access the IIS 10.0 web server IIS Manager. Click the IIS 10.0 web server name. Under IIS, double-click the Logging icon. Verify the Format: under Log File is configured to W3C. Select the Fields button. Under Custom Fields, verify the following fields have been configured: Request Header - Connection Request Header - Warning Server Variable - HTTP_CONNECTION If any of the above fields are not selected, this is a finding.

win-def:appcmdlistconfig_test.comment: Site Default Logfile format must be set to W3C
(Standard Field - User Agent)
Application Request Routing Cache - Proxy Enable Setting
Application Request Routing Cache - Proxy Installation Status

Open the IIS 10.0 Manager. Click the IIS 10.0 web server name. Under IIS, double-click the “MIME Types” icon. From the Group by: drop-down list, select Content Type. From the list of extensions under Application, verify MIME types for OS shell program extensions have been removed, to include at a minimum, the following extensions: .exe .dll .com .bat .csh If any OS shell MIME types are configured, this is a finding.

win-def:registry_test.comment: Verify IIS WebDAV Authoring Rules is not installed via registry test

win-def:file_test.comment: If files with .java or .jpp extensions are found, this is a finding.

win-def:appcmdlistconfig_test.comment: Open the IIS 10.0 Manager. Click the IIS 10.0 web server name. Under "ASP.Net", double-click the "Session State" icon. Under "Cookie Settings", verify the "Mode" has "Use Cookies" selected from the drop-down list. If the "Cookie Settings" "Mode" is not set to "Use Cookies", this is a finding. Alternative method: Click the site name. Select "Configuration Editor" under the "Management" section. From the "Section:" drop-down list at the top of the configuration editor, locate "system.web/sessionState". Verify the "cookieless" is set to "UseCookies". If the "cookieless" is not set to "UseCookies", this is a finding. Note: If IIS 10.0 server/site is used only for system-to-system maintenance, does not allow users to connect to interface, and is restricted to specific system IPs, this is Not Applicable.

(Timeout Setting) Open the IIS 10.0 Manager. Click the IIS 10.0 web server name. Under the "ASP.NET" section, select "Session State". Under "Cookie Settings", verify the "Use Cookies" mode is selected from the "Mode:" drop-down list. Under Time-out (in minutes), verify “20 minutes or less” is selected. If the "Use Cookies” mode is selected and Time-out (in minutes) is configured for “20 minutes or less”, this is not a finding. Alternative method: Click the site name. Select "Configuration Editor" under the "Management" section. From the "Section:" drop-down list at the top of the configuration editor, locate "system.web/sessionState". Verify the "cookieless" is set to "UseCookies". If the "cookieless" is not set to "UseCookies", this is a finding. Note: If IIS 10.0 server/site is used only for system-to-system maintenance, does not allow users to connect to interface, and is restricted to specific system IPs, this is Not Applicable.

(Validation Setting) Open the IIS 10.0 Manager. Click the IIS 10.0 web server name. Double-click the Machine Key icon in the website Home Pane. Verify HMACSHA256 is selected for the Validation method and Auto is selected for the Encryption method. If HMACSHA256 is not selected for the Validation method and/or Auto is not selected for the Encryption method, this is a finding.

(Encryption Method Setting) Open the IIS 10.0 Manager. Click the IIS 10.0 web server name. Double-click the Machine Key icon in the website Home Pane. Verify HMACSHA256 is selected for the Validation method and Auto is selected for the Encryption method. If HMACSHA256 is not selected for the Validation method and/or Auto is not selected for the Encryption method, this is a finding.
Verify Directory Browsing is not enabled
Webserver Error Pages - Detailed errors for local requests and custom error pages for remote requests must be selected
Webserver Error Pages - Detailed errors for local requests and custom error pages for remote requests must be selected

win-def:registry_test.comment: If Enable remote connections is not selected, this is not a finding.

win-def:service_test.comment: This requirement applies to the Web Management Service. If the Web Management Service is not installed, this is Not Applicable.

win-def:fileeffectiverights53_test.comment: (Verify unspecified objects are not assigned to ACL) Open Explorer and navigate to the inetpub directory. Right-click inetpub and select “Properties”. Click the Security tab. Verify the permissions for the following users; if the permissions are less restrictive, this is a finding. System: Full control Administrators: Full control TrustedInstaller: Full control ALL APPLICATION PACKAGES (built-in security group): Read and execute Users: Read and execute, list folder contents Creator/Owner: Special permissions to subkeys. File Perm for Both Physical Paths and Paths with environment variable

(TLS 1.2 Enabled set to 1) Access the IIS 10.0 Web Server. HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\SecurityProviders\SCHANNEL\Protocols\TLS 1.2\Server. Verify a REG_DWORD value of 1 for Enabled.
(TLS 1.1 Disabled) Access the IIS 10.0 Web Server. HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\SecurityProviders\SCHANNEL\Protocols\TLS 1.1\Server. Verify a REG_DWORD value of 0 for Enabled.

(TLS 1.1 DisabledByDefault) Access the IIS 10.0 Web Server. HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\SecurityProviders\SCHANNEL\Protocols\TLS 1.1\Server. Verify a REG_DWORD value of 1 for DisabledByDefault.
(TLS 1.0 Disabled) Access the IIS 10.0 Web Server. HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\SecurityProviders\SCHANNEL\Protocols\TLS 1.0\Server. Verify a REG_DWORD value of 0 for Enabled.

(TLS 1.0 DisabledByDefault) Access the IIS 10.0 Web Server. HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\SecurityProviders\SCHANNEL\Protocols\TLS 1.0\Server. Verify a REG_DWORD value of 1 for DisabledByDefault.
(SSL 2.0 Disabled) Access the IIS 10.0 Web Server. HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\SecurityProviders\SCHANNEL\Protocols\SSL 2.0\Server. Verify a REG_DWORD value of 0 for Enabled.

(SSL 2.0 DisabledByDefault) Access the IIS 10.0 Web Server. HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\SecurityProviders\SCHANNEL\Protocols\SSL 2.0\Server. Verify a REG_DWORD value of 1 for DisabledByDefault.
(SSL 3.0 Disabled) Access the IIS 10.0 Web Server. HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\SecurityProviders\SCHANNEL\Protocols\SSL 3.0\Server. Verify a REG_DWORD value of 0 for Enabled.

(SSL 3.0 DisabledByDefault) Access the IIS 10.0 Web Server. HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\SecurityProviders\SCHANNEL\Protocols\SSL 3.0\Server. Verify a REG_DWORD value of 1 for DisabledByDefault.

Unspecified file extensions must not be allowed to execute on the production web server. (2 of 2)

Ensure "All Users" is set to "Allow", and "Anonymous Users" is set to "Deny" otherwise, this is a finding. If any other rules are present, this is a finding.

Anonymous Users is set to Deny) Click the IIS 10.0 web server name. Double-click the ".NET Authorization Rules" icon. Ensure "All Users" is set to "Allow", and "Anonymous Users" is set to "Deny" otherwise, this is a finding. If any other rules are present, this is a finding.

(No Role Shall Have An Allow) Click the IIS 10.0 web server name. Double-click the ".NET Authorization Rules" icon. Ensure "All Users" is set to "Allow", and "Anonymous Users" is set to "Deny" otherwise, this is a finding. If any other rules are present, this is a finding.

win-def:appcmdlistconfig_test.comment: Access the IIS 10.0 IIS Manager. Click the IIS 10.0 server. Select 'Configuration Editor' under the 'Management' section. From the 'Section:' drop-down list at the top of the configuration editor, locate 'system.applicationHost/sites'. Expand 'siteDefaults'. Expand 'limits'. Review the results and verify the value is greater than zero for the 'maxconnections' parameter. If the maxconnections parameter is set to zero, this is a finding.
Check that system.applicationHost/sites:siteDefaults.hsts.enabled, verify that value equals true.
Check that system.applicationHost/sites:siteDefaults.hsts.includeSubDomains, verify that value is set to True.
Check that system.applicationHost/sites:siteDefaults.hsts.max-age, verify that value is set to 1.
Check that system.applicationHost/sites:siteDefaults.hsts.redirectHttpToHttps, verify that value is set to True.

Under the "Connections" pane on the left side of the management console, select the IIS 10.0 web server.

Click to select the “X-Powered-By” HTTP Header.
If “X-Powered-By” has not been removed, this is a finding.
